name: Route through Villarrobledo — circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Villarrobledo are primarily characterized by the flat plains of La Mancha, with elevations generally ranging between 600 and 800 meters above sea level, offering gentle terrain for running. The city features extensive green zones, including over 140,000 square meters of parks and gardens, such as Parque de Nuestra Señora de la Caridad with its large pond and walkways. The surrounding area includes the Záncara River and its tributaries, along with accessible natural landscapes like the Cerro de la Encantá, providing varied running paths. Best jogging routes around Villarrobledo The most popular jogging route is Route through Villarrobledo — circular, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 8 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with minimal elevation gain, ideal for exploring the city's green spaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Ramón y Cajal Square – Truncated Lives Monument loop from Villarrobledo, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This route guides runners through urban areas and notable landmarks, providing a blend of city and park scenery. Local runners also love the Running loop from Villarrobledo, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through urban green areas, often completed in about 46 minutes. Jogging around Villarrobledo is defined by flat plains, extensive urban parks, and accessible natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops within city parks to moderate trails extending into the surrounding countryside. The routes in Villarrobledo are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Villarrobledo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many jogging routes are available around Villarrobledo?
A: Komoot offers a selection of 10 running routes around Villarrobledo. These trails cater to various preferences, with 9 moderate routes and 1 more challenging option, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Villarrobledo?
A: The running trails in Villarrobledo are primarily characterized by the flat plains of La Mancha, with elevations generally ranging between 600 and 800 meters above sea level. This provides mostly gentle terrain, ideal for comfortable running. You'll find routes through extensive urban parks and accessible natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Villarrobledo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Villarrobledo are circular, offering convenient loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086883003/route-through-villarrobledo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route through Villarrobledo — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.8 miles long and takes about 1 hour 8 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find running paths that pass through parks or green areas?
A: Absolutely. Villarrobledo is known for its extensive green zones, boasting over 140,000 square meters of parks and gardens. Many running paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Villarrobledo&lt;/a&gt;, lead through urban green areas like Parque de Nuestra Señora de la Caridad, Parque Barrio Asturias, and Parque de los Tinajeros, offering pleasant scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging?
A: While jogging, you can explore various natural features and landmarks. Routes often pass through green spaces like Parque de Nuestra Señora de la Caridad with its large pond. The area also features the Záncara River and its tributaries, and the intriguing 'Cerro de la Encantá' (Hill of the Enchanted One), known for its natural beauty and archaeological findings. Within the city, you might pass by highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5934206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de los Tellez&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5934218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, there is one difficult route available for those seeking a greater challenge. Additionally, the region offers longer trails, such as a 13.3-mile hike that includes the Ermita del San Cristóbal and Molino de Estremera, which can be adapted for a longer, more demanding run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Villarrobledo?
A: The running routes in Villarrobledo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with over 50 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often appreciate the flat plains and the accessibility of both urban parks and natural landscapes, making for enjoyable and varied running experiences.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for families?
A: Given the generally flat terrain and numerous well-maintained parks within Villarrobledo, many of the shorter, moderate routes are suitable for families. These paths offer a safe and pleasant environment for all ages to enjoy a run or walk together, especially those within the city's extensive green zones.

Q: Are dog-friendly running trails available in Villarrobledo?
A: Many of the outdoor trails and park areas in Villarrobledo are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. It's always a good idea to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, especially in more populated park areas.

Q: Is parking available near the running trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Villarrobledo, particularly near the city's parks and starting points for many routes. For trails extending into the countryside, you can often find convenient parking options in nearby urban areas or designated spots.

Q: How accessible are the running routes by public transport?
A: Villarrobledo's urban running routes, particularly those within the city's parks and green areas, are easily accessible by public transport or on foot from various points within the city. For routes extending further into the countryside, access might require a short drive or a longer walk from public transport stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Villarrobledo?
A: The best time for running in Villarrobledo is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler temperatures of winter, making for more comfortable outdoor activity.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the running routes?
A: Villarrobledo is a city with various amenities. Many running routes, especially those within or near the urban center, will have cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options within easy reach. For routes venturing further out, it's advisable to plan ahead for refreshments and facilities.
tips: A beautiful park, very green, with lots of vegetation and running paths.

An emblematic place in Villarrobledo that bears the name of the famous Spanish doctor and scientist Santiago Ramón y Cajal, who is considered the father of modern neuroscience. In the square is the church of San Blas, which dates from the 15th and 16th centuries and is a complex construction of Gothic architecture.

The church of San Blas, from the 15th and 16th centuries, is a beautiful example of Gothic, Renaissance and Baroque architecture, which has earned it the declaration of a National Monument, a title obtained in 1977. The oldest part of the building is in the Flamboyant Gothic style, executed with great mastery. The rest was built in the purest Renaissance style and with the stamp of Vandelvira.

The current temple of San Blas stands on a primitive Gothic church from the mid-15th century, at the end of this century it began to be replaced by a new factory. At the foot are remains of the primitive church, of which its western portal stands out, with a magnificent structure made in the Flamboyant Gothic style and made up of an elegant opening with a pointed arch and very slender shapes.
